head: aconter
note: OF aconter (a- + conter < computare); the bare aconter died but the intensive prefixed form raconter is its continuation in the 'tell/recount' sense; conter also survives.

head: acorder
note: OF acorder < Vulgar Latin *accordare; survives as Modern French accorder with modernized (doubled-c) spelling.

head: acraventer
note: OF acraventer/cravanter < *crepantare (crepare); continues as the rare/archaic Modern French lemma acravanter (still in fr.Wiktionary); modern écraser/abattre are unrelated roots.

head: acueillir
note: OF acueillir < accolligere (ad- + colligere); survives as Modern French accueillir with modernized spelling.

head: adeser
note: OF adeser < VL *adhaesare (adhaerere); left no Modern French verb lemma — modern 'touch' uses unrelated toucher, and adhérer is a learned borrowing, not a phonetic descendant.

head: aduber
note: OF aduber/adober < Frankish *dubban; survives as Modern French adouber (to dub a knight), modernized spelling.

head: adurer
note: OF adurer < adurere; still recorded as a vieilli/rare Modern French lemma adurer in fr.Wiktionary, so it continues as itself (not the related endurer/endurcir).

head: afaitier
note: OF afaitier < *affactare (ad- + factum); continues as the technical/falconry Modern French lemma affaiter ('to train/dress'), modernized spelling; the 'arrange' sense shifted.

head: afermer
note: OF afermer < affirmare/firmare; survives as Modern French affermer ('to lease/fix'), modernized spelling continuing the same etymon (affirmer is the learned doublet).

head: afiancer
note: OF afiancer (a- + fiance < fidare); the prefixed form died but the bare descendant fiancer continues the same etymon and pledging sense in Modern French.

head: afiler
note: OF afiler < VL *affilare (ad- + filum); survives as Modern French affiler ('to sharpen'), modernized spelling continuing the same etymon (the 'flow' sense is contextual).

head: afiner
note: OF afiner (a- + fin); survives as Modern French affiner, modernized spelling; the 'finish/achieve' sense gave way to 'refine' but the lemma continues.

head: afubler
note: OF afubler < afibulare (ad- + fibula); survives as Modern French affubler, modernized spelling continuing the same etymon.

head: ajuster
note: OF ajuster < VL *adiuxtare (ad- + iuxta); survives unchanged as Modern French ajuster.

head: anombrer
note: OF anombrer (a- + nombrer < numerare); the prefixed form died but the bare descendant nombrer (literary, 'to count/number') continues the etymon; dénombrer is the common modern relative.

head: aorer
note: OF aorer < adorare; the inherited aorer was supplanted by the re-Latinized learned form adorer continuing the same etymon adorare.

head: appareiller
note: From OF apareillier (VLat *appariculare); survives as modern appareiller (prepare/equip sense) — same lemma, modernized spelling.

head: apparoir
note: From Lat apparere via OF aparoir; modern apparoir is a defective literary verb (il appert) — recognized lemma.

head: aproismier
note: From Lat approximare; inherited OF aproismier left only dialectal forms, the standard verb died. Modern approximer continues the etymon as a relearned/semi-learned form.

head: aquiser
note: OF acoisier/aquiser (Lat quietare/*quietiare, 'to quiet'); continues as the archaic/literary verb accoiser (Littre) — not 'apaiser' (different root).

head: aquiter
note: From OF aquiter (a- + quite, Lat quietus); regular descendant is modern acquitter — same lemma, modernized spelling.

head: ardre
note: From Lat ardere via OF ardre~ardoir; modern ardre survives as an archaic verb (Academie/Wiktionary lemma). Not bruler (replacement root).

head: arester
note: From OF arester (a- + rester, Lat restare); regular descendant is modern arrêter — same lemma, modernized spelling.

head: arguer
note: From Lat arguere; OF arguer continues directly into modern arguer (learned but recognized lemma).

head: aserir
note: OF a-serir 'to become evening/night' (Lat serum 'evening'); left no modern French verb lemma — modern uses unrelated phrasing (tomber la nuit).

head: asmer
note: OVERRIDE: From Lat aestimare; the regular inherited form esmer/asmer died (confused with aimer), replaced by the semi-learned estimer which continues the same etymon. Roland gloss 'oser' is a contextual sense, not the etymon.

head: asseürer
note: OF asseürer (LLat assecurare); old spelling of modern assurer — same lemma.

head: atargier
note: OF a-targier (cf. targier 'tarder', Lat *tardicare); reflexive descendant is modern (s')attarder — same prefixed lemma.

head: avaler
note: OF avaler (a- + val, Lat vallis), orig. 'go down'; continues as modern avaler (sense shifted to 'swallow') — same lemma.

head: avenir
note: From Lat advenire via OF avenir; modern descendant verb is advenir (re-latinized spelling). (avenir survives only as the noun.)

head: aïdier
note: OF aidier (Lat adiutare); regular descendant is modern aider — same lemma.

head: aürer
note: From Lat adorare; inherited OF aorer/aürer died, replaced by re-latinized adorer which continues the same etymon.

head: baillir
note: OF baillir~bailler (Lat bajulare 'to carry'); continues as the archaic/literary verb bailler ('to give') — recognized lemma. Not 'traiter' (different root).

head: baleer
note: OF baleer/baloier 'to float in the wind, sweep'; the form merged phonetically (baloier > balaier > balayer) into modern balayer (sense narrowed to 'sweep').

head: blancheier
note: OF blancheier (blanc + -eier); the -oyer continuation is modern blanchoyer 'to show white' (literary, Littre/Academie lemma). blanchir is a different (-ir) formation.

head: blecier
note: OF blecier/blescier (Frankish *blettjan); regular descendant is modern blesser — same lemma.

head: blesmir
note: OF blesmir (Germanic *blasmi/*blami 'pale'); regular descendant is modern blêmir — same lemma, modernized spelling.

head: bouter
note: OF boter/bouter (Frankish *botan 'to push'); survives as modern bouter (archaic/literary, e.g. 'bouter le feu') — same lemma.

head: brocher
note: OF brochier (broche, Lat brocca); continues as modern brocher — same lemma (the 'spur' sense is archaic but the verb persists).

head: cadeler
note: OF cadeler 'to lead/command' (Occ capdel, Lat capitellum); survives as the archaic/literary verb cadeler (Littre lemma). Not 'commander' (different root).

head: capler
note: OF capler/chapler 'to strike, cut' (Lat capulare); continues as modern chapeler (desuet/regional, Wiktionnaire lemma). Roland gloss 'frapper' is a different etymon (Frankish), NOT the descendant.

head: carier
note: OF carier/charrier (char, Lat carrus); regular descendant is modern charrier — same lemma.

head: castier
note: OF castier > chastier (Lat castigare); regular descendant is modern châtier — same lemma, modernized spelling.

head: chalengier
note: OVERRIDE: From LLat calumniare; inherited OF chalengier (gave English 'challenge') died in French, the etymon continues as the relearned calomnier. Not 'revendiquer' (different root).

head: chaloir
note: From Lat calere via OF chaloir; modern chaloir is a defective impersonal literary verb (il chaut, peu me chaut) — recognized lemma.

head: cheoir
note: From Lat *cadere via OF cheoir; modern descendant is the defective literary verb choir.

head: clamer
note: From Lat clamare via OF clamer; continues directly as modern clamer — same lemma.

head: cliner
note: OF cliner (Lat clinare 'to lean'); the bare verb did not survive into modern French — the etymon's surviving lemma is the prefixed/relearned incliner (Lat inclinare).

head: comperer
note: From Lat comparare via OF comperer; the phonetic descendant lemma is modern comparer (the 'pay dearly' sense was the OF meaning of the same etymon).

head: contrevaloir
note: From Lat contra valere via OF contrevaloir; survives as modern contrevaloir (literary) — same prefixed lemma.

head: cravanter
note: OF cravanter/craventer (Lat crepantare, from crepare); survives as archaic/regional cravanter (Wiktionnaire lemma). Not 's'écrouler' (different root).

head: croller
note: OF croller/crosler (VLat *corrotulare, frequentative of corruere); regular descendant is modern crouler. Roland gloss 'secouer' is a different root, not the descendant.

head: cuarder
note: OF cuarder (denominal from couard 'coward') > modern couarder 'to act like a coward / se couarder', a recognized but rare/literary lemma in Littré, Larousse, fr.Wiktionnaire; matches the Roland sense 'faire le lâche'.

head: cuidier
note: OF cuidier < Latin cogitare 'to think'; survives as modern French cuider 'to think/believe', a recognized lemma (marked obsolete/archaic). Matches the Roland sense 'croire'.

head: cunreer
note: OF conreer/cunreer < Late Latin conredare 'to prepare/arrange' > modern corroyer; descendant survives but now means 'to curry/dress (leather), work (metal/mortar)', not the Roland sense 'équiper'.

head: cuntralier
note: OF contralier/contrarier < Latin contrariare; continues as modern contrarier 'to thwart/annoy'; meaning drifted from the Roland sense 'se quereller' toward 'oppose/vex'.

head: cuvenir
note: OF covenir/cuvenir < Latin convenire (re-Latinized spelling in Middle French) > modern convenir; descendant survives but now means 'to suit/agree', not the Roland impersonal sense 'falloir' (be necessary).

head: decheoir
note: OF decheoir < Vulgar Latin *decadere < Latin decidere; continues directly as modern déchoir 'to decline/fall (in status)'; matches the gloss 'déchoir'.

head: defaillir
note: OF defaillir (dé- + faillir) > modern défaillir 'to faint, weaken, fail'; preserves the Roland sense 'manquer/fail'.

head: degeter
note: OF degeter/desjeter < Latin dejectare (de- + jactare); continues as modern déjeter; descendant survives but now means 'to warp/deform (wood, spine)', not the Roland sense 'rejeter'.

head: demener
note: OF demener (de- + mener) > modern démener (now chiefly reflexive se démener 'to struggle/exert oneself'); meaning shifted from the Roland senses 'conduire/manifester'.

head: derompre
note: OF derompre/derumpre < Latin disrumpere; survives as modern dérompre, a recognized lemma (Littré, lalanguefrancaise) but now restricted/specialized (agriculture, falconry, papermaking), not the general Roland sense 'rompre'.

head: derumpre
note: Variant spelling of derompre; OF derumpre < Latin disrumpere > modern dérompre, a recognized but specialized lemma; meaning narrowed away from the Roland sense 'rompre'.

head: deschevalcier
note: OF deschevalchier (des- + chevalchier) > modern déchevaucher, a rare recognized lemma (CNRTL, fr.Wiktionnaire) 'to dismount / separate (bones)'; meaning drifted from the Roland sense 'désarçonner'.

head: desclore
note: OF desclore < Vulgar Latin *disclaudere < Latin discludere; continues as modern déclore 'to open/unclose', a recognized (archaic, defective) lemma; matches the Roland sense 'ouvrir/fendre'.

head: desconfire
note: OF desconfire (dis- + confire) > modern déconfire; descendant survives but the live sense is now 'to discombobulate' (archaic 'to destroy'), drifted from the Roland sense 'défaire/rout'.

head: descoudre
note: OF descoudre (des- + coudre) > modern découdre 'to unstitch/unsew'; preserves the Roland sense 'découdre'.

head: desfaire
note: OF desfaire < Early Medieval Latin disfacere > Middle French desfaire > modern défaire 'to undo/take apart'; preserves the Roland sense 'défaire'.

head: desherberger
note: OF desherbergier (des- + herbergier 'to lodge') left no recognized modern French verb lemma; 'desherberger' is not in modern dictionaries (modern equivalent 'déloger/lever le camp' is a different formation).

head: desmailler
note: OF desmailler (des- + maille) continues as modern démailler 'to unravel/unmesh'; the Roland sense 'break the chainmail (maille)' is the same root sense applied to armor.

head: desotreier
note: OF desotroier/desotreier (des- + otroier 'to grant') left no modern French verb lemma; 'désoctroyer' is not a recognized modern lemma (only octroyer survives, the positive form).

head: desrengier
note: OF desrengier (des- + rengier 'to put in line') > modern déranger; descendant survives but now means 'to disturb/disarrange', drifted from the Roland sense 'quitter les rangs' (the original 11th-c. sense per CNRTL).

head: dessevrer
note: OF dessevrer < Vulgar Latin *disseperare left no modern French verb lemma (it survived only into English 'dissever'); modern French sevrer descends from the unprefixed OF sevrer, a different etymon.

head: destorber
note: OF destorber < Latin disturbare left no modern French verb lemma (it survived only into English 'disturb'); modern French uses unrelated roots troubler/déranger for the Roland sense 'empêcher'.

head: detordre
note: OF detordre (de- + tordre) > modern détordre 'to untwist'; preserves the Roland twisting sense 'tordre' (de- nuance of un-twisting).

head: detrenchier
note: OF detrenchier (de- + trenchier) > modern détrancher, a marginal recognized lemma (Cordial) 'to cut up'; matches the Roland sense 'taillader'; low confidence as it is barely lexicalized in modern dictionaries.

head: doluser
note: OF doloser/dolouser < Latin dolere did not survive into modern French as a verb lemma; the related OF doloir is itself archaic/defunct and only the adjective dolent persists, so no modern verb continues this exact etymon.

head: duire
note: OF duire < Latin ducere 'to lead'; survives as modern duire, an obsolete/literary lemma meaning 'to train (esp. a bird)'; meaning shifted from the Roland sense 'lisser' (to smooth).

head: démontrer
note: Latin demonstrare; head is already the modern spelling (OF demustrer), a living modern French verb lemma — direct descendant.

head: départir
note: Latin dispartire/de+partir; head is the modern spelling, still a recognized (literary) modern French verb lemma — direct descendant.

head: embattre
note: OF embatre (en+batre) survives as modern French embattre (post-1990 spelling of embatre), a recognized verb lemma — direct descendant.

head: embruncher
note: OF embruncher, variant of embroncher; embroncher survives as a technical modern French verb (roofing/carpentry) — phonetic descendant of the same etymon.

head: empeindre
note: OF empeindre 'to push/strike' < Latin impingere left no modern French verb lemma (modern uses pousser); not to be confused with empreindre < imprimere, a different etymon.

head: empleier
note: OF empleier/emploiier < Latin implicare; regular descendant is modern French employer.

head: enchacier
note: OF enchacier 'to pursue' (en+chacier) left no modern verb lemma in that sense (modern uses pourchasser/chasser); modern enchâsser is a homograph from en+châsse, a different etymon.

head: encombrer
note: OF encombrer (en+combre < Gaulish *comboros); survives unchanged as the modern French verb encombrer — direct descendant.

head: encontrer
note: OF encontrer < Late Latin incontrare; survives as the literary modern French verb encontrer (alongside the prefixed rencontrer) — direct descendant.

head: engigner
note: OF engignier 'to deceive' < Latin ingeniare; survives as the archaic/literary modern French verb engeigner — direct descendant.

head: enhaitier
note: OF enhaitier 'to encourage' (en+hait, of Germanic origin) left no modern French verb lemma; modern uses encourager (unrelated root).

head: enhelder
note: OF enhelder 'to fit a hilt' (from Germanic helt 'sword hilt') left no modern French verb lemma; the verb died with Old French.

head: enluminer
note: OF enluminer < Latin inluminare; survives unchanged as the modern French verb enluminer — direct descendant.

head: enveisier
note: OF enveisier/envoisier 'to enjoy oneself' (< Latin invitiare) left no modern French verb lemma (only the adjective envoisié lingered); modern uses s'amuser.

head: envoluper
note: OF envoluper/envoleper; regular descendant is modern French envelopper (same etymon, modernized form).

head: errer
note: OF errer (the 'travel' sense < Latin iterare, merged with errer < errare); errer survives as a modern French verb lemma — direct descendant.

head: esbaldir
note: OF (s')esbaldir (es+bald, Germanic) > esbaudir > modern (s')ébaudir, an archaic/literary modern French verb lemma — direct descendant.

head: esbaneier
note: OF esbaneier/esbanoier 'to amuse oneself' did not survive past Middle French (esbanoyer); no modern French verb lemma; modern uses s'amuser.

head: escanteler
note: OF escanteler 'to break in pieces' (es+cantel) left no modern French verb lemma; modern écarteler is a different root (quartier), modern uses mettre en pièces.

head: eschiver
note: OF eschiver (< Frankish *skiuhan); Wiktionary lists modern French esquiver as its descendant, though via a Spanish-loan detour (eschiver > Sp. esquivar > Fr. esquiver) rather than a straight native phonetic line.

head: esclairier
note: OF esclairier < Latin exclariare; regular descendant is modern French éclairer (same etymon, modernized form).

head: esclargir
note: OF esclargir/esclarcir < Latin exclaricire; regular descendant is modern French éclaircir (same etymon, modernized form).

head: esclicier
note: OF esclicier 'to fly into splinters' (< Frankish *slitan); regular descendant is modern French éclisser (same etymon; sense narrowed to 'to splint').

head: escolorer
note: OF escolorer 'to lose color/turn pale' (es+colorer) left no modern French verb lemma in that sense; modern uses pâlir/se décolorer.

head: escrier
note: OF escrier < es+crier; regular descendant is modern French (s')écrier (same etymon, modernized form).

head: esculter
note: OF esculter/escouter < Latin auscultare; regular descendant is modern French écouter (same etymon).

head: esdemetre
note: OF esdemetre 'to dash/rush forward' (es+de+metre) left no modern French verb lemma; modern uses s'élancer (different root).

head: esfreer
note: OF esfreer/effreer (< Vulgar Latin *exfridare, Germanic frid); regular descendant is modern French effrayer (same etymon).

head: esgarder
note: OF esgarder 'to look at' (es+garder, Germanic wardon) left no surviving modern French verb lemma; the es- form died and modern French uses regarder (re+garder), a different-prefix formation, not a descendant of esgarder.

head: esgarer
note: OF esgarer (es+garer, Germanic); regular descendant is modern French égarer (same etymon).

head: eslegier
note: OF eslegier 'to acquit at law' from ex- + Latin litigare; etymon left no modern French verb lemma (modern uses unrelated acquitter/payer).

head: eslire
note: OF eslire (Vulgar Latin exlegere, Latin eligere) → MF eslire → French élire; es- prefix > é-.

head: esmaier
note: OF esmaier only survives as the noun émoi (via MF esmoy); no modern French verb descendant lemma.

head: esmerer
note: OF esmerer 'to refine/purify' (Latin exmerare) left only the participle esmeré; no surviving modern French verb lemma (synonym affiner is a different root).

head: espaenter
note: Spelling variant of OF espoenter (Vulgar Latin *expaventare); → French épouvanter.

head: espargnier
note: OF espargnier (Frankish *sparanjan) → French épargner; es- prefix > é-.

head: espier
note: OF espier (Frankish *spehon) → MF espier → French épier (espionner is a separate later formation).

head: esploitier
note: OF esploitier/espleitier (Latin explicitare) → French exploiter.

head: espoenter
note: OF espoenter (Vulgar Latin *expaventare) → French épouvanter.

head: esragier
note: OF esragier is an alternative form of enragier (en- + rage); → French enrager.

head: establer
note: OF establer (Latin stabulare) → French établer 'put in a stable' (archaic/agricultural lemma, still in dictionaries).

head: ester
note: OF ester (Latin stare) → French ester, surviving as a rare/archaic legal verb ('ester en justice').

head: estordre
note: OF estordre (Latin extorquere) 'wrench away/escape'; popular form left no modern verb lemma (étordre is not modern; extorquer is a later learned re-borrowing, échapper unrelated).

head: estovoir
note: OF estovoir, impersonal 'to be necessary' (Latin est opus + -oir); died out leaving no modern French verb lemma (modern uses unrelated falloir).

head: estraire
note: OF estraire (Latin extrahere) → French extraire.

head: estrosser
note: OF estrosser/estrousser (es- + trousser, Vulgar Latin *torsare); prefixed form died, the root continues as French trousser.

head: ferir
note: OF ferir (Latin ferire) → French férir, the defective literary verb (sans coup férir); frapper is a different etymon.

head: finer
note: OF finer (Latin finare/finire) → MF finer/finir → French finir continues the etymon.

head: forsfaire
note: OF forsfaire (fors + faire, Latin foris facere) → French forfaire, the archaic/literary defective verb.

head: fraindre
note: OF fraindre (Latin frangere); bare form died, but the prefixed inherited descendant enfreindre (OF enfraindre) continues the etymon.

head: fruisier
note: OF fruisier/froissier (Vulgar Latin *frustiare, from frustum) → French froisser.

head: gaber
note: OF gaber (Old Norse gabba) → French gaber, surviving (archaic/literary) verb.

head: garder
note: OF guarder/garder (Frankish *wardon) → French garder unchanged.

head: garir
note: OF garir/guarir (Frankish *warjan) → French guérir.

head: gesir
note: OF gesir (Latin iacere) → French gésir, the defective verb (ci-gît).

head: gracier
note: OF gracier 'give thanks' (Medieval Latin gratiare) → French gracier; lemma survives, sense shifted to 'pardon'.

head: guagier
note: OF guagier/gagier (Frankish *waddi) → French gager.

head: guarir
note: OF guarir/garir (Frankish *warjan) → French guérir.

head: guaster
note: OF guaster/gaster (Latin vastare crossed with Frankish *wostjan) → French gâter.

head: guerpir
note: OF guerpir (Frankish *werpjan); bare form survives only in legal use, the prefixed descendant déguerpir is the living continuation.

head: guier
note: OF guier (Frankish *witan) → French guider (with -d- reinforced by Italian guidare); same etymon line.

head: gésir
note: OF gesir (Latin iacere) → French gésir, the defective verb; same head as 'gesir'.

head: honnir
note: OF honir (Frankish *haunjan) → MF honnir → French honnir, archaic/literary.

head: hosteler
note: OF hosteler (from ostel/hostel, Medieval Latin hospitale) → French hôteler 'to lodge', a rare/archaic dictionary lemma; héberger is a different (Germanic) root.

head: irascir
note: OF irascir/iraistre (Latin irasci); only the learned adjective irascible survives, no modern French verb lemma (modern uses s'irriter/se fâcher, different roots).

head: issir
note: OF issir (Latin exire) → French issir, archaic but still a dictionary lemma (participle issu common).

head: joster
note: OF joster < VL *iuxtare; Wiktionary Descendants -> French jouter (to joust); the modernized continuant.

head: jouster
note: Variant spelling of joster < *iuxtare; same descendant jouter.

head: loer
note: OF loer < Latin locare (to hire/let); regular descendant is modern louer (rent), which also merges laudare 'praise'.

head: luer
note: Variant of OF loer < locare 'to hire/pay'; descendant is modern louer.

head: manoir
note: OF verb manoir < Latin manere survives only as the noun manoir; no modern French verb lemma (meaning now rester/demeurer).

head: mesler
note: OF mesler < VL *misculare; Wiktionary Descendants -> French mêler (s->circumflex); regular descendant.

head: mespenser
note: OF mes- + penser 'to think ill'; the mes- prefixed verb left no modern lemma (modern uses mal penser); penser survives but is a different unprefixed verb.

head: mouvoir
note: OF movoir/mouvoir < Latin movere; survives unchanged as modern mouvoir.

head: muer
note: OF muer < Latin mutare; survives as modern muer (to moult; the 'ne pouvoir muer' sense is archaic but same lemma).

head: nasfrer
note: OF nafrer/navrer/nasfrer 'to wound' (prob. < Old Norse nafra); regular descendant is modern navrer.

head: neier
note: OF neier < Latin necare; modernized spelling is noyer (to drown).

head: noncier
note: OF noncier < Latin nuntiare; the bare verb (*noncer) died out; modern uses prefixed annoncer/prononcer (anuntiare/pronuntiare), different etyma -> no bare descendant.

head: ocire
note: OF ocire < VL *occidere; survives as the defective archaic/literary verb occire (infinitive + past part. occis).

head: osteier
note: OF osteier/ostoier 'to wage war', denominal of ost 'army'; left no modern verb lemma (modern uses guerroyer).

head: otreier
note: OF otreier/otroiier < Latin auctorizare; Wiktionary: otroiier -> octroyer with learned re-insertion of c; regular descendant.

head: ouïr
note: OF oir/ouir < Latin audire; survives as the defective literary verb ouïr; same lemma.

head: oïr
note: OF oïr < Latin audire; modern continuant is the defective literary verb ouïr.

head: palmeier
note: OF palmeier/palmoiier 'to brandish' (denominal of palme 'palm of hand'); left no modern verb lemma (modern uses brandir).

head: peceier
note: OF peceier/pecoiier 'to break in pieces' (denominal of piece); bare form died, but the prefix-preserving continuant dépecer (dé- + piece) survives as the modern descendant of the pecier family.

head: peler
note: OF peler < Latin pilare/pellis; survives unchanged as modern peler (to peel/strip).

head: plevir
note: OF plevir 'to pledge' (Frankish origin); no standard modern French verb lemma (only rare legal pleiger, a different derivation); modern uses garantir/jurer.

head: plorer
note: OF plorer < Latin plorare; modernized form is pleurer; regular descendant.

head: poindre
note: OF poindre < Latin pungere; survives as modern poindre (to dawn/sting), defective/literary; same lemma.

head: porparler
note: OF porparler 'to plot/negotiate'; the verb died, surviving only as the noun pourparler(s); no modern verb lemma.

head: porpenser
note: OF porpenser (por- + penser) 'to ponder'; the por-/pour- prefixed verb left no modern lemma (modern uses réfléchir).

head: porprendre
note: OF porprendre (por- + prendre) 'to occupy/surround'; archaic, survives only via past part. noun pourpris; no modern verb lemma.

head: preer
note: OF preer/preder < LL praedare 'to plunder'; left no modern verb lemma (gives noun proie; modern uses piller).

head: priser
note: OF prisier < LL pretiare; survives as modern priser (to prize/value); same lemma.

head: querre
note: OF querre < Latin quaerere; modern continuant is the defective literary quérir (to fetch); same etymon.

head: quérir
note: OF querre/querir < quaerere; modern defective literary verb quérir; same lemma.

head: rachater
note: OF rachater (re- + achater) < re- + accaptare; modernized form is racheter; regular descendant (gloss 'echo' is contextual).

head: raiier
note: OF raiier/raier < Latin radiare 'to radiate/stream'; Wiktionary Descendants -> French rayer (noted 'possibly'); the direct phonetic descendant.

head: reconoistre
note: OF reconoistre (re- + conoistre) < recognoscere; modernized spelling reconnaître; regular descendant.

head: recroire
note: OF recroire (re- + croire) < recredere; survives as the rare modern verb recroire (Wiktionary lists it as a current French 3rd-group lemma); same etymon.

head: recuvrer
note: OF recuvrer/recovrer < Latin recuperare; modernized spelling recouvrer (to recover); regular descendant.

head: redrecer
note: OF redrecier (re- + drecier) < *directiare; modernized form redresser; regular descendant.

head: referir
note: re-+ferir (L. ferire); the simple etymon survives as defective literary 'férir', but the prefixed 'referir' left no modern French lemma.

head: reflamber
note: re-+flamber (Frankish *flamber/L. flammare); 'reflamber' itself is not a standard modern lemma, but the etymon continues in the living verb 'flamber'.

head: remaindre
note: OF remaindre/remanoir (L. remanere) is only an Old French lemma; the etymon died in standard modern French, which uses unrelated rester/demeurer.

head: remembrer
note: L. rememorari/re-+membrer; 'remembrer' survives as an archaic/literary modern French verb 'to remember', same spelling continuation.

head: repairer
note: OF repairier (L. repatriare); continues as modern French 'repairer' (also se repairer, of an animal returning to its lair), same etymon.

head: retraire
note: re-+traire (L. retrahere); 'retraire' is a recognized archaic/legal modern French verb, regular phonetic continuation of the etymon.

head: rover
note: OF rover (L. rogare 'to ask'); descent traced only to Middle French rover, no surviving modern French verb lemma.

head: saillir
note: L. salire 'to leap/gush'; survives directly as modern French 'saillir', same spelling and etymon (the 'gush/spurt' sense matches the Roland 'jaillir').

head: se démenter
note: OF (se) dementer (LL. dementare 'drive mad'); no modern lemma survives — modern 'démentir' is a different etymon (de-+mentir); meaning now via unrelated se lamenter.

head: se herberger
note: OF (se) herberger (Frankish *heribergon); continues as modern French 'héberger', regular phonetic descendant of the same etymon.

head: se merveiller
note: OF (se) merveillier (L. mirabilia/*mirabiliare); reflexive 'se merveiller' is archaic, but the etymon survives in the living prefixed verb (s')émerveiller.

head: se porpenser
note: OF (se) porpenser (por-+penser, L. pensare); the prefixed verb died, leaving no modern lemma — modern uses simple penser/réfléchir.

head: seignier
note: OF seignier 'to make the sign of the cross/bless' (L. signare); continues (as a refection on 'signe') in modern 'signer', whose reflexive 'se signer' = cross oneself, matching the Roland sense.

head: seiner
note: OF seiner/saignier (L. sanguinare 'to bleed'); regular phonetic descendant is modern French 'saigner', matching the Roland sense.

head: semondre
note: L. summonere; survives as the archaic/literary modern French verb 'semondre' ('to invite/summon'), same spelling continuation.

head: senefier
note: L. significare; OF inherited senefier was refashioned as the learned modern French 'signifier', continuing the same etymon (the Roland sense 'signifier' matches).

head: seoir
note: L. sedere; survives as the modern French verb 'seoir' (to suit/be seated), same spelling continuation of the etymon.

head: sevrer
note: OF sevrer (VL. *seperare, variant of L. separare); survives directly as modern French 'sevrer' ('to wean'), inherited doublet of borrowed séparer.

head: sigler
note: OF sigler (Old Norse sigla 'to sail'); regular descendant via Middle French singler to modern French 'cingler' (to sail/scud along).

head: sivre
note: OF sivre (VL. *sequere, L. sequi); modern descendant is 'suivre', same etymon with modernized spelling.

head: soleir
note: L. solere 'to be accustomed'; survives as the obsolete/archaic modern French verb lemma 'souloir', regular descendant of the etymon.

head: sommer
note: OF somer 'to load a pack animal' (from somme < LL. sauma/L. sagma); this verbal sense left no modern lemma — modern 'sommer' is a different formation (somme 'sum'/'summon').

head: soner
note: L. sonare; survives directly as modern French 'sonner' (here glossed 'dire/utter'), same etymon with doubled-n spelling.

head: sourdre
note: OF sourdre (L. surgere); survives as the defective/formal modern French verb 'sourdre' ('to well up'), doublet of surgir.

head: targier
note: OVERRIDE: OF targier/tarder 'to delay' (VL. *tardicare, from L. tardare); the 'delay' verb survives in modern 'tarder' (a sibling formation), while OF targier left no direct same-form lemma — modern 'se targuer' is unrelated.

head: tenser
note: OVERRIDE: OF tenser/tencier (VL. *tentiare, L. tentus); the OF verb's surviving continuation is modern 'tancer' (to scold), though the Roland 'protéger' sense (L. tensare) did not survive.

head: tentir
note: OF tentir (*tinnitire, from L. tinnire); the simple verb died, but the etymon survives only in the prefixed modern French verb 'retentir' (re-+tentir).

head: tolir
note: OF tolir/toldre (L. tollere 'to take away'); descent traced only to Middle French tollir, no surviving modern French lemma — modern uses unrelated enlever/ôter.

head: traire
note: L. trahere 'to pull/draw'; survives as modern French 'traire' (now specialized to 'to milk'), same etymon; Roland sense 'tirer' is the original draw/pull meaning.

head: tramettre
note: OF tramettre (L. transmittere); refashioned learnedly as modern French 'transmettre', continuing the same etymon (Roland sense 'transmettre' matches).

head: tresaler
note: OF tresaler (tres-+aler, L. trans+ambulare, 'to pass away/expire'); only an Old/Middle French lemma, no modern French verb — the link to tressaillir is unrelated (tres-+saillir).

head: tresturner
note: OF tresturner (tres-+turner, L. trans+tornare); the prefixed verb died, leaving no modern lemma — only the simple 'tourner' survives.

head: venteler
note: OF venteler (L. ventilare, der. of vent); survives as an archaic modern French verb lemma 'venteler' ('to flutter in the wind'), per fr.Wiktionary, same spelling.

head: voloir
note: OF voloir (VL. *volere, L. velle); modern descendant is 'vouloir', same etymon with modernized spelling.
